EnterpriseSDK
Table of Contents

Interface IField

Namespace
ESRI.ArcGIS.Geodatabase
Assembly
ESRI.Server.Geodatabase.dll

Provides access to members that return information about the field.

[Guid("FCB01CB3-9F0B-11D0-BEC7-00805F7C4268")]
[InterfaceType(ComInterfaceType.InterfaceIsIUnknown)]
public interface IField

Properties

AliasName

The alias name of the field.

string AliasName { get; }

Property Value

string

Examples

Sample Snippet

DefaultValue

The default value of the field.

object DefaultValue { get; }

Property Value

object

Examples

Sample Snippet

Domain

The default domain of the field.

IDomain Domain { get; }

Property Value

IDomain

Examples

Sample Snippet

DomainFixed

Indicates if the field's domain is fixed.

bool DomainFixed { get; }

Property Value

bool

Examples

Sample Snippet

Editable

Indicates if the field is editable.

bool Editable { get; }

Property Value

bool

Examples

Sample Snippet

GeometryDef

The geometry definition for the field if IsGeometry is TRUE.

IGeometryDef GeometryDef { get; }

Property Value

IGeometryDef

Examples

Sample Snippet

Remarks

<P>The <STRONG>GeometryDef</STRONG> object defines the spatial qualities of a feature class. The most fundmental spatial quality is the Geometry type, for example, point, line, and polygon. Other information necessary to define the feature class includes the spatial referencing system, whether the vertices have height or measure data, and, for geodatabases, the spatial index parameters.</P> <P>The <STRONG>GeometryDef </STRONG>can be accessed from a shape field, that is a field of type <STRONG>esriFieldTypeGeometry</STRONG>. The actual geometry type is defined by the <STRONG>esriGeometryType </STRONG>enumeration, though currently only four values are acceptable with respect to <STRONG>GeometryDef </STRONG>objects: <STRONG>esriGeometryPoint</STRONG>, <STRONG>esriGeometryMultipoint</STRONG>, <STRONG>esriGeometryPolyline</STRONG>, and <STRONG>esriGeometryPolygon</STRONG>.</P> <P>Beware of the confusion between the <STRONG>esriGeometryType</STRONG> enumeration and the similarly named <STRONG>esriFeatureType</STRONG> and <STRONG>esriShapeType</STRONG>. The following table shows the different roles of those enumerations.</P> <DIV class=tablediv> <TABLE class=dtTABLE cellSpacing=0> <TBODY> <TR vAlign=top> <TH class=t width="33%">ArcObjects Enumeration</TH> <TH class=t width="33%">Values</TH> <TH class=t width="33%">Interfaces that use the enumeration</TH></TR> <TR vAlign=top> <TD width="33%"><STRONG>esriGeometryType</STRONG><BR><BR>Type of geometry in a geodatabase. Those in bold are valid for feature classes, others are component geometry objects.</TD> <TD class=t width="33%"><STRONG>esriGeometryPoint<BR>esriGeometryMultipoint<BR>esriGeometryPolygon<BR>esriGeometryPolyline<BR>esriGeometryLine</STRONG><BR>esriGeometryCircularArc<BR>esriGeometryEllipticArc<BR>esriGeometryBezier3Curve<BR>esriGeometryPath<BR>esriGeometryNull<BR>esriGeometryRing<BR>esriGeometryEnvelope<BR>esriGeometryAny<BR>esriGeometryBag<BR>esriGeometryMultiPatch<BR>esriGeometryTriangleStrip<BR>esriGeometryTriangleFan<BR>esriGeometryRay<BR>esriGeometrySphere<BR>esriGeometryTriangles<BR></TD> <TD width="33%">IFeatureClass::ShapeType<BR>IGeometry::GeometryType<BR>IGeometryDef::GeometryType<BR><BR>also<BR>IGeometryDefEdit,<BR>IGeometryFactory,<BR>IEditSketchExtension,<BR>IGridConversionOp,<BR>IDimensionConstructor</TD></TR> <TR vAlign=top> <TD width="33%"><STRONG>esriFeatureType</STRONG><BR><BR>Feature types defined by<BR>feature class extensions</TD> <TD class=t width="33%">esriFTSimple<BR>esriFTSimpleJunction<BR>esriFTSimpleEdge<BR>esriFTComplexJunction<BR>esriFTComplexEdge<BR>esriFTAnnotation<BR>esriFTCoverageAnnotation<BR>esriFTDimension<BR>esriFTRasterCatalogItem<BR></TD> <TD width="33%">IFeature::FeatureType<BR>IFeatureClass::FeatureType<BR>IFeatureClassDescription::FeatureType<BR>IFeatureWorkspace::CreateFeatureClass<BR>IFeatureDataset::CreateFeatureClass<BR><BR>also, IGeometricNetwork and<BR>INetworkLoader</TD></TR> <TR vAlign=top> <TD width="33%"><STRONG>esriShapeType</STRONG><BR><BR>Type of geometry in a<BR>shapefile</TD> <TD class=t width="33%">See the help for esriShapeType<BR>for a full list </TD> <TD width="33%">IFeature::FeatureType<BR>IFeatureClass::FeatureType<BR>IFeatureClassDescription::FeatureType<BR>IFeatureWorkspace::CreateFeatureClass<BR>IFeatureDataset::CreateFeatureClass<BR><BR>also, IGeometricNetwork and<BR>INetworkLoader</TD></TR></TBODY></TABLE></DIV>

IsNullable

Indicates if the field can contain null values.

bool IsNullable { get; }

Property Value

bool

Sample Snippet

Length

The maximum length, in bytes, for values described by the field.

int Length { get; }

Property Value

int

Examples

Sample Snippet

Name

The name of the field.

string Name { get; }

Property Value

string

Examples

Sample Snippet

Precision

The precision for field values.

int Precision { get; }

Property Value

int

Examples

Sample Snippet

RasterDef

The raster column definition.

IRasterDef RasterDef { get; }

Property Value

IRasterDef

Examples

Sample Snippet

Remarks

<P>The <STRONG>RasterDef</STRONG> object specifies the property of a raster column, such as description of the column, spatial reference, and for Personal and File geodatabases, whether the Raster field is managed by the geodatabase.</P>

Required

Indicates if the field is required.

bool Required { get; }

Property Value

bool

Examples

Sample Snippet

Scale

The scale for field values.

int Scale { get; }

Property Value

int

Examples

Sample Snippet

Type

The type of the field.

esriFieldType Type { get; }

esriFieldType

VarType

The VARTYPE of the field (e.g. VT_I4).

int VarType { get; }

int

CheckValue(object)

Indicates if the value is valid given the field definition.

bool CheckValue(object Value)
